metadata.title: Paediatric Addendum on the CHMP Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of acute heart failure

rules.rule_text: This addendum must be read in conjunction with the adult Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Acute Heart Failure (CHMP/EWP/2986/03 Rev. 1).
rules.verbatim_quote: This is an addendum to the Guideline on Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products in the Treatment of Acute Heart Failure (CHMP/EWP/2986/03 Rev. 1) for adults and should be read in conjunction with this guideline.

rules.rule_text: Guidance applies to the design and conduct of studies intended for use in children of all ages (0-18 years) when developing products for paediatric AHF.
rules.verbatim_quote: Guidance is therefore included on the design and conduct of studies intended for use in children of all ages (0-18 years) when developing products for AHF.
rules.exceptions: Aspects relating to surgical treatment such as correction of congenital defects and mechanical support are beyond scope.

rules.rule_text: Composite endpoints combining several parameters are recommended when sample size is limited in paediatric AHF trials. Components should be chosen carefully to capture the spectrum of relevant clinical parameters.
rules.verbatim_quote: Combination of several parameters as a composite offers advantages when sample size is limited. The components should be chosen carefully to capture the spectrum of relevant clinical parameters.

rules.rule_text: Composite endpoints must ensure directional concordance of the components. When centre-specific differences of care are common, composites must include parameters sensitive to such differences.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is advantageous to ensure directional concordance of the components. When centre specific differences of care are common, composite endpoints could be challenging particularly those composites that include parameters sensitive to such differences and should be carefully addressed.

rules.rule_text: Applicants and sponsors should seek scientific advice from the CHMP if use of ranked or more sensitive composite endpoints is foreseen in paediatric AHF trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Ranked composite endpoints may offer certain advantages and a need to develop more sensitive composite endpoints is recognised. Applicants and sponsors are advised to seek scientific advice if use of such endpoints is foreseen.

rules.rule_text: When evaluating mortality as an endpoint in paediatric AHF, sudden death (or death due to arrhythmia) must be included, and post mortem examination may be required to confirm absence of other causes.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is important to include sudden death (or death due to arrhythmia when documented) in evaluating mortality. On occasion, in cases of sudden death, there will be need for confirmation of absence of other causes and this may include a post mortem examination.

rules.rule_text: Definitions of all mortality parameters (all-cause death, cardiovascular death, sudden death) must be clear and objectively evaluated in paediatric AHF trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: There should be clarity in the definitions of each of these parameters and they should be objectively evaluated.

rules.rule_text: Time to referral for transplantation is considered a more useful endpoint than time to actual transplantation, and should use objective and pre-specified criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: Time to referral for transplantation using objective and pre-specified criteria is considered the more useful indicator as time to actual transplantation is dependent of many factors including geographical location and organ availability.

rules.rule_text: Use of ventricular assist devices must be recorded as both short-term bridge to transplantation and long-term treatment modality.
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of Ventricular assist devices both as a short term bridge (to cardiac transplantation) or as long term treatment modality should be recorded.

rules.rule_text: Echocardiography must be performed following a pre-specified protocol and analysed by a blinded, centralised laboratory with trained observers/readers.
rules.verbatim_quote: Echocardiography should be performed following a pre-specified protocol and analysed by a blinded, centralised laboratory with trained observers/readers.
rules.exceptions: Central adjudication may be necessary when blinded reading in a centralised facility has not been deployed.

rules.rule_text: In multicentre trials, standardised training must be provided to recording technicians, and interobserver as well as intraobserver variability must be evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: With multicentre trials, it is also important that standardised training is provided to the recording technicians and, interobserver as well as intraobserver variability are evaluated to permit a robust assessment of left ventricular function.

rules.rule_text: Clinical scoring systems used for efficacy assessment must be validated and consistent. The choice of scoring system (NYHA, Ross, PHFI) must be defined a priori and adequately justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in clinical scores could be useful as measures of efficacy provided their use is validated and consistent. ... It is recommended that the choice should be defined a priori and adequately justified.

rules.rule_text: Haemodynamic measures in paediatric AHF must be linked to clinical outcome measures such as mortality or removal of the need for transplantation, not just surrogate parameters.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is important to link the medicinal product's effect on haemodynamic measures to clinical outcome measures such as mortality or removal of the need for transplantation.

rules.rule_text: Diagnosis of paediatric AHF must be based on baseline evaluation of functional or clinical scoring systems, combined with imaging such as echocardiographic parameters or cardiac MRI to establish structural abnormalities.
rules.verbatim_quote: The criteria and diagnosis of AHF should be based on baseline evaluation of functional or clinical scoring systems, combined with imaging such as echocardiographic parameters or cardiac MRI to establish structural abnormalities.

rules.rule_text: When patients with heart failure presenting after palliative or corrective surgery are included in clinical studies, the parameters for defining heart failure must be clearly laid out in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: When these patients are included in clinical studies, care should be taken to ensure that the parameters for defining heart failure are clearly laid out in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Aetiology of heart failure varies by age, gender and ethnic background; stratification should be used to separate patients based on different pathophysiological states.
rules.verbatim_quote: The aetiology of HF may vary depending on age, gender and ethnic background. This should be accounted for and accommodated. ... ideally some form of stratification may be necessary to separate patients based on the different pathophysiological states.
rules.exceptions: Due to small numbers, distinct studies in different aetiologies may not be possible.

rules.rule_text: PK/PD modelling based on adult data should be used to explore pharmacokinetic behaviour in children, determine the need for specific paediatric studies, and optimise study design.
rules.verbatim_quote: Where possible, use of PK/PD modelling based on data derived from adult populations should be performed to explore the pharmacokinetic behaviour in children to determine the need for specific studies, and to optimize the design of these studies.

rules.rule_text: Special paediatric formulations must be developed as appropriate for different age groups (infants, young children, and adolescents).
rules.verbatim_quote: There is likely to be a necessity to develop special paediatric formulations as appropriate for different age groups (infants, young children and adolescents).

rules.rule_text: When confirmatory trials are placebo controlled, an add-on design to the best standard of care is recommended, with demonstration of clear superiority in terms of efficacy and safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: When confirmatory trials are placebo controlled, an add-on design to the best standard of care is recommended. In such studies, demonstration of clear superiority in terms of efficacy and safety should be the aim.
rules.exceptions: Use of an appropriate comparator is encouraged when placebo controlled studies are not feasible due to variability of patient groups and treatment practices.

rules.rule_text: Safety evaluation in paediatric AHF must include age-appropriate parameters: hypotension or low BP, hypoperfusion, arrhythmias, failure to thrive, growth retardation, delays in neuro-motor and neurocognitive development, and renal function measures.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety evaluation in paediatric AHF is expected to be generally similar to adults with additional parameters (or endpoints) that are important in children. These include parameters such as hypotension or low BP (using age-appropriate definitions), hypoperfusion, arrhythmias, in addition to failure to thrive, growth retardation or delays in neuro-motor and neurocognitive development and may all be relevant safety end-points.
